html:
Act III

Scene: HelenaтАЩs drawing-room as before. The room is dark and gray. The steel shutters which are outside are still closed as at the end of Act II. Alquist is sitting in chair down stage at extreme R. Domin comes into the room, L. 2. Subdued voices. Dr.┬аGall is looking out of the window at Center. He is seated in a chair.

Domin Gets binoculars from desk; crosses up to window. To Gall. Any more of them?
Dr.┬аGall Yes. There standing like a wall, beyond the garden railing. Why are they so quiet? ItтАЩs monstrous to be besieged with silence.
Domin Looking through the barred windows. I should like to know what they are waiting for? They must make a start any minute now. If they lean against the railings it will snap like a match.
Dr.┬аGall They arenтАЩt armed.
Domin Puzzled. We couldnтАЩt hold our own for five minutes. Man alive, they overwhelm us like an avalanche. Why donтАЩt they make a rush for it? I say. Turns to Gall.
Dr.┬аGall Well?
Domin IтАЩd like to know what will become of us in the next ten minutes. TheyтАЩve got us in a vise. WeтАЩre done for, Gall.
Dr.┬аGall You know, we made one serious mistake.
Domin What?
Dr.┬аGall We made the RobotsтАЩ faces too much alike. A hundred thousand faces all alike, all facing this way. A hundred thousand expressionless bubbles. ItтАЩs like a nightmare.
Domin You think if theyтАЩd been differentтБатАФ
Dr.┬аGall It wouldnтАЩt have been such an awful sight!
Domin Looks through binoculars towards the harbor. IтАЩd like to know what theyтАЩre unloading from the Amelia.
Dr.┬аGall Not firearms.
Fabry Enters L. 2 with a plug-box to which is attached a long cable or wire. Hallemeier following him. Fabry attaches the cable to an electric installation which is on the floor near the wall, down stage at L. 1 entrance. All right, Hallemeier, lay down that wire.
Hallemeier Just inside the room. That was a bit of work. WhatтАЩs the news? Seeing Domin and Gall at the window.
Dr.┬аGall WeтАЩre completely surrounded.
Hallemeier Crosses to window.) WeтАЩve barricaded the passages and the stairs. Going to window. God, what swarms of them. I donтАЩt like the looks of them, Domin. ThereтАЩs a feeling of death about it all. Any water here?
Fabry Ready!
Dr.┬аGall Turning round in the chair. WhatтАЩs that wire for, Fabry?
Fabry The electrical installation. Now we can run the current all along the garden railing. Up to window. Whenever we like. If anyone touches it heтАЩll know it. WeтАЩve still got some people there anyhow.
Dr.┬аGall Where?
Fabry In the electrical works. At least, I hope so. Goes to lamp on table L. C. and turns on lamp. Ah, theyтАЩre there, and theyтАЩre working. As long as thatтАЩll burn weтАЩre all right. To window.
Hallemeier The barricades are all right, too, Fabry.
Fabry Your barricades! I can put twelve hundred volts into that railing. Helena is playing RachmaninoffтАЩs тАЬElegieтАЭ off L. 1.
Domin WhereтАЩs Busman? Domin has left window and is walking up and down stage across front.
Fabry Downstairs in the office. HeтАЩs working out some calculations.
Domin IтАЩve called him. We must have a conference. Crosses to L.
Alquist Thank God Madame Helena can still play. Hallemeier crosses to L. 1 door, opens it slightly and listens to music. Enter Busman L. 2.
Fabry Look out, BusтБатАФlook out for the wires.
Dr.┬аGall WhatтАЩs that youтАЩre carrying?
Busman Laying the books on the table L. C. The ledger, my boy. IтАЩd like to wind up the accounts beforeтБатАФbeforeтБатАФDomin crosses up to window. Well, this time I shanтАЩt wait till the New Year to strike a balance. WhatтАЩs up? Goes to window. Absolutely quiet.
Dr.┬аGall CanтАЩt you see anything?
Busman Nothing but blueтБатАФblue everywhere.
Dr.┬аGall ThatтАЩs the Robots.
Domin The Robots are unloading firearms from the Amelia.
Busman Well, what of it? How can I stop them? Returns to L. C. table, sits and opens ledger.
Domin We canтАЩt stop them.
Busman Then let me go on with my accounts. Goes on with his work.
Domin Picks up telescope. Good God! The Ultimus has trained her guns on us.
Dr.┬аGall WhoтАЩs done that?
Domin The Robots on board.
Fabry HтАЩm, then of courseтБатАФPause. ThenтБатАФthen thatтАЩs the end of us. To R. corner of desk.
Dr.┬аGall You mean?
Fabry The Robots are practised marksmen.
Domin Yes. ItтАЩs inevitable. Pause.
Dr.┬аGall Swinging around; looking into room. Pause. That was criminal of old Europe to teach the Robots to fight. Damn them. CouldnтАЩt they have given us a rest with their politics? It was a crime to make soldiers of them.
Alquist It was a crime to make Robots.
Domin Quietly. Down C. No, Alquist, I donтАЩt regret that even today.
Alquist Not even today?
Domin Dreamily. Not even today, the last day of civilization. It was a colossal achievement.
Busman Sotto voce. Three hundred sixty million.
Domin From window. Alquist, this is our last hour. We are already speaking half in the other world. That was not an evil dream to shatter the servitude of labor. The dreadful and humiliating labor that man had to undergo. Work was too hard. Life was too hard. And to overcome thatтБатАФ
Alquist Was not what the two Rossums dreamed of. Old Rossum only thought of his Godless tricks, and the young one of his milliards. And thatтАЩs not what your R.U.R. shareholders dream of either. They dream of dividends, and their dividends are the ruin of mankind.
Domin To Hell with your dividends. Crossing R. in front of couch. Do you suppose IтАЩd have done an hourтАЩs work for them? It was for myself that I worked, for my own satisfaction. I wanted man to become the master. So that he shouldnтАЩt live merely for the crust of bread. I wanted not a single soul to be broken by other peopleтАЩs machinery. I wanted nothing, nothing, nothing to be left of this appalling social structure. IтАЩm revolted by poverty. I wanted a new generation. I wantedтБатАФI thoughtтБатАФ
Alquist Well?
Domin Front of couch. I wanted to turn the whole of mankind into an aristocracy of the world. An aristocracy nourished by millions of mechanical slaves. Unrestricted, free and consummated in man. And maybe more than man.
Alquist Superman?
Domin Yes. Oh, only to have a hundred years of time. Another hundred years for the future of mankind.
Busman Sotto voce. Carried forwardтБатАФfour hundred and twenty millions. Domin sits on couch.
Hallemeier PausesтБатАФback of couch. What a fine thing music is. We ought to have gone in for that before.
Fabry Gone in for what?
Hallemeier Beauty, lovely things. What a lot of lovely things there are. The world was wonderful, and weтБатАФwe hereтБатАФtell me, what enjoyment did we have?
Busman Sotto voce. Five hundred and twenty million.
Hallemeier Life was a good thing, life wasтБатАФLooking out of window. Directly to Fabry. Fabry, switch the current into that railing.
Fabry Why? Rushes to electric installation at L.
Hallemeier TheyтАЩre grabbing hold of it. Domin risesтБатАФstraightens up. All rise.
Dr.┬аGall Connect it up.
Hallemeier Fine, thatтАЩs doubled them up. Two, three, four killed.
Dr.┬аGall TheyтАЩre retreating. Domin sits.
Hallemeier Five killed.
Dr.┬аGall Pause. The first encounter.
Hallemeier TheyтАЩre charred to cinders, my boy. Who says we must give in? Music stops.
Domin Alquist and Gall sit. Wiping his forehead. Perhaps weтАЩve been killed this hundred years and are only ghosts. ItтАЩs as if I had been through all this before, as if IтАЩd already had a mortal wound here in the throat. Looking at each as he speaks. And you, Fabry, had once been shot in the head. And you, Gall, torn limb from limb. And Hallemeier knifed.
Hallemeier Fancy me being knifed. Looks at each. Then speaks. Why are you so quiet, you fools? Steps down. Speak, canтАЩt you?
Alquist And who is to blame for all this?
Hallemeier Nobody is to blame except the Robots.
Alquist No, it is we are to blame. You, Domin, myselfтБатАФall of us. For our own selfish ends, for profit, for progress, we have destroyed mankind. Now weтАЩll burst with all our greatness.
Hallemeier Rubbish, man. Mankind canтАЩt be wiped out so easily.
Alquist ItтАЩs our fault. ItтАЩs our fault. Rises, coming R. of Gall.
Dr.┬аGall No! IтАЩm to blame for this, for everything thatтАЩs happened. He leaves the window and comes down to end of couch.
Fabry You, Gall?
Dr.┬аGall I changed the Robots.
Busman WhatтАЩs that?
Dr.┬аGall I changed the character of the Robots. I changed the way of making them. Just a few details about their bodies. ChieflyтБатАФchiefly, theirтБатАФtheir irritability.
Hallemeier Damn it, why?
Busman What did you do it for?
Fabry Why didnтАЩt you say anything?
Dr.┬аGall I did it in secret. I was transforming them into human beings. In certain respects theyтАЩre already above us. TheyтАЩre stronger than we are.
Fabry And whatтАЩs that got to do with the revolt of the Robots?
Dr.┬аGall Everything, in my opinion. TheyтАЩve ceased to be machines. TheyтАЩre already aware of their superiority, and they hate us as they hate everything human.
Domin Perhaps weтАЩre only phantoms.
Fabry Stop, Harry. We havenтАЩt much time, Doctor Gall.
Domin Fabry, Fabry, how your forehead bleeds where the shot pierced it.
Fabry Crosses to Gall. Be silent! Doctor Gall, you admit changing the way of making the Robots.
Dr.┬аGall Yes.
Fabry Were you aware of what might be the consequences of your experiment?
Dr.┬аGall I was bound to reckon with such a possibility.
Fabry Amusing. Why did you do it, then?
Helena enters L. 1.
Dr.┬аGall For my own satisfaction. The experiment was my own.
Helena ThatтАЩs not true, Doctor Gall! Crosses to couch.
Domin Rises. Helena, you? Crosses to her. LetтАЩs look at you. Oh, itтАЩs terrible to be dead. He rises and crushes her in his arms.
Helena Stop, Harry.
Domin No, no, Helena, donтАЩt leave me now. You are life itself.
Helena No, dear, I wonтАЩt leave you. But I must tell them. Doctor Gall is not guilty.
Fabry Excuse me. Gall was under certain obligations.
Helena No. He did it because I wanted it. Tell them, Doctor GallтБатАФhow many years ago did I ask you toтБатАФ?
Dr.┬аGall I did it on my own responsibility.
Helena DonтАЩt believe him. I asked him to give the Robots souls.
Domin This has nothing to do with the soul.
Helena ThatтАЩs what he said. He said that he could change only a physiologicalтБатАФa physiologicalтБатАФ
Hallemeier From up at window. A physiological correlate?
Helena Yes. But it meant so much to me that he should do even that.
Domin Why?
Helena I thought that if they were more like us they would understand us better. That they couldnтАЩt hate us if they were only a little more human.
Domin Nobody can hate man more than man.
Helena Oh, donтАЩt speak like that, Harry. It was so terrible, this cruel strangeness between us and them. ThatтАЩs why I asked Gall to change the Robots. I swear to you that he didnтАЩt want to.
Domin But he did it.
Helena Because I asked him.
Dr.┬аGall I did it for myself as an experiment. Up to window.
Helena No, Doctor Gall! I know you wouldnтАЩt refuse me.
Domin Why?
Helena You know, Harry.
Domin Yes, because heтАЩs in love with youтБатАФlike all of them. Fabry up to window. Pause. Domin takes her in his arms.
Hallemeier Good God, theyтАЩre sprouting up out of the earth. Why, perhaps these very walls will change into Robots.
Busman Rises; crosses to Gall. Gall, when did you actually start these tricks of yours?
Dr.┬аGall Three years ago.
Busman Aha. And on how many Robots altogether did you carry out your improvements? Walking to and fro.
Dr.┬аGall A few hundred of them.
Busman Ah! That means for every million of the good old Robots thereтАЩs only one of GallтАЩs improved pattern. Back to table L. C.
Domin What of it? Crossing around L., he stands upstage in the L. 2 doorway.
Busman That itтАЩs of no consequence whatsoever.
Fabry BusmanтАЩs right. Helena sits in armchair R. of L. C. table.
Busman I should think so, my boy; but do you know what is to blame for this lovely mess?
Fabry What?
Busman The number! Crosses to L. of L. C. table. Upon my soul, we might have known that some day or other the Robots would be stronger than human beings, and that this was bound to happen. And we were doing all we could to bring it about as soon as possible. You, Domin, you, Fabry, myselfтБатАФ
Domin Are you accusing us? Turning on him.
Busman Oh, do you suppose the management controls the output? ItтАЩs the demand that controls the output.
Helena And is it for that we must perish?
Busman ThatтАЩs a nasty word, Madame Helena. We donтАЩt want to perish. I donтАЩt, anyhow. He sits L. of table.
Domin No? What do you want to do?
Busman I want to get out of this, thatтАЩs all.
Domin Oh, stop it, Busman.
Busman Seriously, Harry, I think we might try it.
Domin How? To front again.
Busman By fair means. I do everything by fair means. Give me a free hand and IтАЩll negotiate with the Robots.
Domin By fair means?
Busman Rises. Of course. For instance, IтАЩll say to them: тАЬWorthy and Worshipful Robots, you have everything. You have intellect, you have power, you have firearms. But we have just one interesting screed, a dirty old yellow scrap of paperтБатАФтАЭ
Domin RossumтАЩs manuscript? Interest from All. Gall is at C., near couch. Hallemeier is up at window C.
Busman Yes. тАЬAnd that,тАЭ IтАЩll tell them, тАЬcontains an account of your illustrious origin, the noble process of your manufacture and so on. Worthy Robots, without this scribble on that paper you will not be able to produce a single new colleague. In another twenty years there will not be the living specimen of a Robot whom you could exhibit in a menagerie. My esteemed friends, that would be a great blow to you, but if you will let all of us human beings on RossumтАЩs Island go on board that ship we will deliver the factory and the secret of the process to you in return. You allow us to get away, and we will allow you to manufacture yourselves. That, worthy Robots, is a fair deal. Something for something.тАЭ ThatтАЩs what IтАЩd say to them, my boys. Sits.
Domin Crosses to C. Busman, do you think weтАЩd sell the manuscript?
Busman Yes, I do. If not in a friendly way, thenтБатАФeither we sell it or theyтАЩll find it. Just as you like.
Domin Busman, we can destroy RossumтАЩs manuscript.
Busman Then we destroy everythingтБатАФnot only the manuscript but ourselves. Just as you think fit.
Domin There are over thirty of us on this island. Are we to sell the secret? And save that many souls at the risk of enslaving mankindтБатАФ
Busman Why, youтАЩre mad. WhoтАЩd sell the whole manuscript?
Domin Busman, no cheating! To L. C. table.
Busman Well then, sell, but afterwardsтБатАФ
Domin Well?
Busman LetтАЩs suppose this happens. When weтАЩre on board the Ultimus IтАЩll stop up my ears with cotton wool, lie down somewhere in the hold, and youтАЩll train the guns on the factory and blow it to smithereens, and with it RossumтАЩs secret.
Fabry Rises. No!
Domin Busman, youтАЩre noтБатАФgentleman. If we sell them it will be a straight sale.
Busman Rises. ItтАЩs in the interest of humanity toтБатАФ
Domin ItтАЩs in the interest of humanity to keep our wordтБатАФ
Hallemeier Oh, come, what rubbish!
Domin This is a fearful decision. We are selling the destiny of mankind. Are we to sell or destroy? Fabry?
Fabry Sell.
Domin Gall?
Dr.┬аGall Sell.
Domin Hallemeier?
Hallemeier Sell, of course.
Domin Alquist?
Alquist As God wills.
Domin Starts off R. Very well, gentlemen.
Helena Harry, youтАЩre not asking me.
Domin Stops. To her. No, child. Starting R. DonтАЩt you worry about it. He pats her shoulder.
Fabry WhoтАЩll do the negotiating?
Busman I will. Up to window.
Domin Wait till I bring the manuscript. Domin goes out R.
Helena Rises. Harry, donтАЩt go! Helena sits. All look at her. Pause.
Fabry Looking out of window. Oh, to escape you! youтБатАФmatterтБатАФin revolt; oh, to preserve human life, if only upon a single vesselтБатАФ
Dr.┬аGall DonтАЩt be afraid. Going to back of couch. Madame Helena. WeтАЩll sail far away from here; weтАЩll begin life all over again.
Helena Oh, Gall, donтАЩt speak.
Fabry Crosses to L. of Gall. It isnтАЩt too late. Going to L. of her chair. It will be a little State with one ship. Alquist will build us a house and you shall rule over us.
Hallemeier Crosses to L. of Fabry. Madame Helena, FabryтАЩs right.
Helena Breaking down. Oh, stop! Stop!
Busman Good! Crosses to L. of L. C. table. I donтАЩt mind beginning all over again. That suits me right down to the ground. Going through papers on table.
Fabry And this little State of ours could be the center of future life. A place of refuge where we could gather strength. Why, in a few hundred years we could conquer the world again.
Alquist You believe that even today?
Fabry Yes!
Busman Amen. You see, Madame Helena, weтАЩre not so badly off.
Domin Storms into R. To R. of couch. Hoarsely. WhereтАЩs old RossumтАЩs manuscript? To R. C.
Busman In your strongbox, of course.
Domin SomeoneтБатАФhasтБатАФstolen it!
Dr.┬аGall Impossible.
Domin Who has stolen it?
Helena Standing up. I did. Reactions from Fabry and Hallemeier.
Domin Where did you put it?
Helena Harry, IтАЩll tell you everything. Only forgive me.
Domin Where did you put it?
Helena Pointing to fireplace. This morningтБатАФI burntтБатАФthe two copies.
Domin Burnt them? WhereтБатАФin the fireplace? Goes to fireplace, followed by Fabry, Hallemeier and Busman.
Helena Throwing herself on her knees. By sofa, facing upstage. For HeavenтАЩs sake, Harry.
Domin Going to fireplace. NothingтБатАФnothing but ashes. Wait, whatтАЩs this? Picks out a charred piece of paper and reads, тАЬBy adding.тАЭ Fabry, Gall and Hallemeier move up to him.
Dr.┬аGall LetтАЩs see. тАЬBy adding biogen toтБатАФтАЭ ThatтАЩs all.
Domin Is that part of it?
Dr.┬аGall Carrying paper down and letting it fall. Yes. Gall crosses to L. C. Hallemeier to R. of L. C. table; Fabry to window; Busman to L. of L. C. table.
Busman God in Heaven! Sits L. of table.
Domin Then weтАЩre done for. Get up, Helena.
Helena Then youтАЩve forgiven me?
Domin Get up, child. I canтАЩt bearтБатАФ
Fabry Lifting her up. Please donтАЩt torture us.
Helena Harry, what have I done?
Fabry Coming to Helena. DonтАЩt, Madame Helena.
Domin Takes Helena to couch. She sits. Gall, you couldnтАЩt draw up RossumтАЩs formula from memory?
Dr.┬аGall ItтАЩs out of the question. Even with my recent experiments, I couldnтАЩt work without referring to the formulaтБатАФAt L. C. ItтАЩs extremely complicated.
Domin Try. All our lives depend upon it.
Dr.┬аGall Without experiments itтАЩs impossible.
Domin And with experiments?
Dr.┬аGall It might take years. Besides, IтАЩm not old Rossum.
Busman God in Heaven! God in Heaven!
Domin Up to fireplace. So then this was the greatest triumph of the human intellect. These ashes.
Helena Harry, what have I done?
Domin Comes to her. Why did you burn it?
Helena I have destroyed you.
Busman God in Heaven!
Domin Sits R. of her. Helena, why did you do it, dear?
Helena I wanted all of us to go away. I wanted to put an end to the factory and everything. It was so awful.
Domin What was awful?
Helena That children had stopped being born. Because human beings were not needed to do the work of the world. ThatтАЩs whyтБатАФ
Domin Is that what you were thinking of? Well, perhaps in your own way you are right.
Busman Wait a bit. Rising. Good God, what a fool I am not to have thought of it before.
Hallemeier What?
Busman Five hundred and twenty millions in banknotes and checks. Half a billion in our safe. TheyтАЩll sell for half a billionтБатАФfor half a billion theyтАЩllтБатАФCrosses to Domin.
Dr.┬аGall Are you mad, Busman?
Busman I may not be a gentleman, but for a half a billionтБатАФCrosses back to L.
Domin Where are you going? Gall clutches Busman.
Busman Leave me alone. Leave me alone! Good God, for half a billion anything can be bought. Gall and Hallemeier after him, then stop. He rushes out L. 2. Fabry, Gall and Hallemeier to window.
Fabry They stand there as if turned to stoneтБатАФwaiting as if something dreadful could be wrought by their silenceтБатАФ
Hallemeier Looking out window. The spirit of the mob.
Fabry Yes. It hovers above them like a quivering of the air.
Helena Oh, God! Doctor Gall, this is ghastly!
Fabry There is nothing more terrible than the mob. The one in front is their leader. Domin crosses to window.
Helena Rises. Which one? Rushing to window.
Hallemeier Point him out.
Fabry L. window. The one at the edge of the dock. This morning I saw him talking to the sailors in the harbor.
Helena Doctor Gall, thatтАЩs Radius. Backing into the room, horror-stricken.
Dr.┬аGall Yes.
Domin Radius! Radius!
Hallemeier Could you get him from here, Fabry?
Fabry I hope so.
Hallemeier Try it, then.
Fabry GoodтБатАФDraws his revolver and takes his aim.
Helena To Fabry. Fabry, donтАЩt shoot him.
Fabry HeтАЩs their leader.
Dr.┬аGall Fire! Standing above table L. C.
Helena Fabry, I beg of you. She goes to Fabry and holds his arm.
Fabry Pause. Lowering the revolver. Very well.
Domin It was RadiusтАЩ life I spared.
Dr.┬аGall Do you think that a Robot can be grateful? Pause.
Fabry BusmanтАЩs going out to them.
Hallemeier HeтАЩs carrying something. Papers. ThatтАЩs money. Bundles of money. WhatтАЩs that for?
Domin Surely he doesnтАЩt want to sell his life. He rushes to window C. Busman, have you gone mad?
Fabry HeтАЩs running up to the railing. Busman. Busman.
Hallemeier Yelling. Busman, come back.
Fabry HeтАЩs talking to the Robots. HeтАЩs showing them the money.
Hallemeier HeтАЩs pointing to us.
Helena He wants to buy us off.
Fabry HeтАЩd better not touch the railing.
Hallemeier Now heтАЩs waving his arms about.
Domin Busman, come back!
Fabry Busman, keep away from that railing. DonтАЩt touch it, damn you. Quick, switch off the current. Domin runs to L. Helena screams and All drop back from the window. The current has killed him.
Alquist Pause. The first one. Still in chair down R. Helena sits in chair at window.
Fabry Dead, with half a billion by his side. Crosses down to table L. C.
Hallemeier All honor to him. He wanted to buy us life. Crosses to chair L. Pause. Wind machine begins.
Dr.┬аGall Do you hear?
Domin A roaring. Like a wind. To L.
Dr.┬аGall Like a storm.
Fabry Lighting the table lamp at table L. C. The dynamo is still goingтБатАФour people are still there.
Hallemeier It was a great thing to be a man. Facing lamp from up C. There was something immense about it.
Fabry Facing the lamp. From manтАЩs thought and manтАЩs power came this light, our last hope. Leaning over lamp.
Hallemeier Facing lamp. ManтАЩs power! May it keep watch over us. Leaning over lamp.
Alquist Facing lamp. ManтАЩs power.
Domin At corner of table down L. C. Facing lamp. Yes! A torch to be given from hand to hand from age to age forever! (The lamp goes out. Explosions.)
Hallemeier The end.
Fabry The electric works have fallen! Terrific Explosions outside. More Explosions.
Domin In here, Helena. He takes Helena off through door R. and reenters. Now quickly! WhoтАЩll be on the lower doorway?
Dr.┬аGall I will. Rushes out L. 2.
Domin Near couch. Who on the stairs?
Fabry I will. You go with her. Going out L. 2.
Domin The ante room?
Alquist I will. He rises and goes toward the L. 1.
Domin Have you got a revolver?
Alquist Yes, but I wonтАЩt shoot.
Domin What will you do, then?
Alquist Going out L. 1 Die.
Hallemeier IтАЩll stay here. Explosions. Rapid firing of machine gun from below. Go to her, Harry.
Domin Yes, in a second. Gets from fireplace and examines two Browning guns.
(Warn Curtain)
Hallemeier Confound it, go to her.
Domin Goodbye. Exits R.
Hallemeier Alone. Now for a barricade quickly! Drags an armchair, sofa and table to R. door. The damned devils, theyтАЩve got bombs. I must put up a defense. Even ifтБатАФeven ifтБатАФDonтАЩt give in, Gall. As he builds his barricade. I mustnтАЩt give inтБатАФwithoutтБатАФaтБатАФstruggle. A Robot enters through windows at back. The Robot jumps down from balcony and stabs Hallemeier in the back. Enter Radius from balcony.
Robot Standing up from prostrate form of Hallemeier. Yes. Other Robots enter from all doors. A revolver shot off L.
Radius Finished them allтБатАФ
Robots Yes, yes, yes.
Two Robots Dragging in Alquist L. 1. He didnтАЩt shoot. Shall we kill him?
Radius No. Leave him!
Robot He is a man!
Radius He works with his hands like the Robots.
Alquist Kill me.
